title: Spectral Features for MIP Decomposition Selection: A Computational Study with the First Complete MIPLIB 2017 Decomposition Census

primary.name: Spectral Feature Family
primary.description: Eight spectral features from the constraint hypergraph Laplacian for MIP instance characterization. Continuous, geometry-aware, with formal permutation-invariance (F1) and scaling-sensitivity analysis (F2). Validated via feature-count-controlled ablation.
primary.novelty: Spectral features for decomposition selection are new. Graph Laplacian features exist in ML4CO but not hypergraph Laplacian features specifically designed for block-structure detection with theoretical connection to decomposition bounds via L3.

primary.name: MIPLIB 2017 Decomposition Census
primary.description: Spectral structural annotations for all 1,065 instances. Decomposition evaluation (Benders via SCIP-native, DW via GCG) on 500 stratified instances at cutoffs {60s, 300s, 900s, 3600s}. Released as open Parquet dataset.
primary.novelty: First systematic cross-method decomposition evaluation on the standard MIP benchmark. Prior work (Bergner et al. 2015, Kruber et al. 2017) covers DW-only via GCG.

primary.name: Lemma L3 and Specializations (Partition-to-Bound Bridge)
primary.description: Formal bound on LP-vs-decomposed-dual gap in terms of crossing hyperedge weight. Method-specific specializations L3-C for Benders (reduced-cost weighting) and DW (linking-constraint dual weighting). Standalone value as partition quality metric.
primary.novelty: The core bound formalizes known Lagrangian duality principles (Fisher 1981, Geoffrion 1974) in hypergraph language with (n_e-1) multi-block factor. The L3-C specializations are the most novel math items. The universal partition quality metric framing is new.

secondary.name: Spectral Futility Predictor
secondary.description: Empirically calibrated binary classifier predicting when no k-block decomposition improves dual bound beyond tolerance epsilon. NOT a formal certificate.

secondary.name: Reformulation Selection Framing
secondary.description: Formal distinction between algorithm selection (which solver?) and reformulation selection (which mathematical object?). Extends Kruber et al. 2017 from DW-only to cross-method.

conditional.name: Lightweight 2-Method Oracle (C-lite)
conditional.description: Binary classifiers for Benders/DW-amenability with spectral partition injection into GCG (.dec) and SCIP Benders. Activated only if G3 passes strongly (>=8pp).

motivational.name: Proposition T2 (Spectral Scaling Law)
motivational.description: z_LP - z_D(pi_hat) <= C * delta^2/gamma^2 where C = O(k * kappa^4 * ||c||_inf). Vacuous on 60-70% of MIPLIB. Motivational only (<=2 pages).

constraint_hypergraph.definition: Given A in R^{m x n}, H(A) = (V, E, w) with V = {1,...,n} (variables), E = {e_1,...,e_m} where e_i = {j : A_{ij} != 0}, w_i = ||A_tilde_{i,:}||_2^2 / d_i after equilibration.
constraint_hypergraph.notes: Constraints = hyperedges. Empty constraints removed post-presolve.

clique_expansion_laplacian.definition: L_CE = D - W, W_{jj'} = sum_{i: j,j' in e_i} w_i/(d_i-1), D = diag(W*1_n). Each hyperedge induces weighted clique.

incidence_matrix_laplacian.definition: L_I = D_v - H*W_e*D_e^{-1}*H^T (Bolla 1993, Zhou et al. 2006). H is n x m binary incidence matrix.
incidence_matrix_laplacian.validation: Spearman rho > 0.85 with clique-expansion on overlap instances

normalized_laplacian.note: Symmetric normalization pinned consistently across all results.

spectral_gap.k_way_note: For k-way partitioning, relevant gap is gamma_k = lambda_{k+1} - lambda_k. Feature SF2 uses gamma_2 as conservative proxy.

coupling_energy.note: Depends on chosen partition. Computed after spectral clustering.

crossing_weight.note: RETROSPECTIVE metric requiring LP dual y*. For prospective use: approximate duals from limited simplex iterations.

dual_bound_gap.clarification: LP gap (not IP gap). z_D is the decomposed dual bound with finite columns/cuts, not the converged value.

spectral_features.name: Spectral gap ratio
spectral_features.inconsistency_note: Uses gamma_2; T2 needs gamma_k for k>2. Conservative proxy.

spectral_features.name: Fiedler vector localization entropy
spectral_features.edge_case: Not unique when lambda_2 has multiplicity > 1. Average over degenerate eigenspace.

spectral_features.name: Block separability index
spectral_features.note: Silhouette on VV^T rows. k-means++ seed=42, 10 restarts.

k_selection.sensitivity: Report features at k in {2, 5, 10, 20} as robustness check

L3.name: Partition-to-Bound Bridge
L3.statement: For any partition P = {B_1,...,B_k} of variables, z_LP - z_D(P) <= sum_{e in E_cross(P)} |y*_e| * (n_e(P) - 1)
assumptions: A1: LP relaxation has finite optimal solution
A3: Dual y* exists and is finite
L3.proof_technique: LP duality -> constraint partition -> coupling relaxation -> weak duality gap bounded by dual multiplier magnitudes on crossing constraints
L3.honest_novelty: Formalizes Fisher/Geoffrion Lagrangian duality in hypergraph language. (n_e-1) factor and universal partition metric framing are new.
known_issues: Well-known core principle: relationship to standard Lagrangian bounds
Bounds LP gap, not IP gap

L3_sp.name: Spectral Partition Bound
L3_sp.statement: For spectral partition P*: W_cross(P*) <= O(delta^2 * d_max / gamma_k^2) * max_e |y*_e|
L3_sp.proof_technique: Davis-Kahan sin Theta -> eigenspace angle O(delta/gamma_k) -> NJW rounding -> misclassification O(delta^2/gamma_k^2) -> crossing weight
known_issues: d_max factor vacuous on dense constraints
Different forms for clique-expansion vs incidence Laplacian
gamma_k vs gamma_2 inconsistency with Feature SF2

L3_C_benders.name: L3-C Benders Specialization
L3_C_benders.statement: z_LP - z_Benders^(t) <= sum_{j coupling} |r_j^(t)| * |blocks(j)| (reduced-cost weighting at iteration t)
L3_C_benders.note: Most novel: bridges variable partition (Benders) with constraint partition (L3)

L3_C_dw.name: L3-C DW Specialization
L3_C_dw.statement: z_LP - z_DW^(t) <= sum_{i linking} |mu_i^(t)| * (|blocks(i)| - 1) (DW master duals)
L3_C_dw.note: (|blocks(i)|-1) tightens generic (k-1) factor. Directly evaluates GCG partitions.

T2.name: Spectral Scaling Law (Motivational)
T2.statement: z_LP - z_D(pi_hat) <= C * delta^2/gamma^2, C = O(k * kappa^4 * ||c||_inf)
T2.proof_chain: Davis-Kahan -> O(delta/gamma) eigenspace angle -> O(delta^2/gamma^2) misclassification -> L3 -> bound degradation
T2.kappa4_origin: kappa^2 from Hoffman bound + kappa^2 from constraint magnitude conversion
T2.vacuousness: C vacuous for kappa > 10^3 (60-70% of MIPLIB)

F1.name: Permutation Invariance
F1.statement: All 8 spectral features invariant under simultaneous row/column permutation of A
F1.caveat: SF4 not unique when lambda_2 has multiplicity > 1; SF7 requires fixed k-means seed

F2.name: Scaling Sensitivity (Corrected)
F2.limitation: Only bounds SF1 (gamma_2), not all 8 features. Others validated empirically.

proof_dependency_dag.independence: F1, F2 independent of L3/T2. If T2 fails, everything else survives. If L3 fails, theoretical contribution collapses.

alg1_laplacian.name: Hypergraph Laplacian Construction
alg1_laplacian.input: Sparse A in R^{m x n} (post-presolve)

alg4_refinement.name: Method-Aware Partition Refinement (C-lite)
steps: Greedy local search: move variables to minimize L3 bound

evaluation.core_experiment: Feature ablation: 6 configs x 3 top-k x 3 classifiers with nested 5x3 CV
evaluation.labels: {Benders, DW, neither} from external baselines at wall-clock parity

kill_gates.kill_threshold: All of rho<0.5, acc<65%, precision<80% fail

critical_addressed: L3 retrospective nature: acknowledged, framed as quality metric, approximate duals for prospective use
SF2/T2 gamma_2 vs gamma_k inconsistency: acknowledged, gamma_2 as conservative proxy, report both
k-selection circularity: eigengap heuristic with sensitivity analysis at k in {2,5,10,20}
Feature discontinuity at d_max=200: include d_max as control, consider uniform incidence Laplacian
T2 perturbation model circularity: already motivational only, delta^2/gamma^2 validated empirically
L3 novelty vs LP duality: honest framing with specific new elements identified
Near-zero gamma_2: NaN sentinel, disconnected-instance detection
Spectral=proxy for density: G0 with nonlinear RF regression, mRMR feature selection
killer_questions_prepared: Q1: How is L3 different from standard Lagrangian relaxation gap? A: Hypergraph formalization + (n_e-1) + universal metric + L3-C specializations
Q2: Are spectral features just expensive proxies for density? A: G0 gate + controlled ablation + mRMR
Q3: Why not GNNs? A: Interpretability + theoretical grounding + O(nnz*k) vs O(nnz*d) + L3 connection
Q4: How is k selected? A: Eigengap heuristic + sensitivity analysis + reported per instance
Q5: Is the census useful if 75%+ is none? A: The 25% IS the finding; futility predictor covers the rest
